column19: Scheme Twists in the Villain Deck

column23: Was able to get cables rare Hawkeyes rare very early with some lucky draws of Skids and then after that it took a few turns to KO the grays and after that the Mandarin was cooked duck….also helped I got 5 rings out early

So, i was playing fine, thinning my decks, and then hand1 (yellow/green) played 34 cards in a turn, 12 of which were recruited that turn, generated almost 60 attack and killed everything. Oh well.
The second try i wanted to make it even better, but hq strongly disagreed, and i abandoned it on turn 30 (had less vp already).

Horrific deck shuffles after my very good false start (I forgot the additional +2\\\ for the henchmen) quit both games much too fast. The HQ had 4 Bystanders in my first correct try at the beginning. Guess what the first card was? TWIST - this game was crap. The other game was terrific again. Very bad timed twists and 3 Strikes in my first 7 turns meant Wounds right from the start. It shouldn't been, was lucky enough in my previous games. I'm probably out after this - so thank you for this nice tourney! :-)

Second Game was horrible. First was pretty great. So this gets a 3/5. Kylah was able to fight the Jean rare in the city and use it to get the Iron Man one for free before a twist snatched her away.  Ollie made a decent blue deck using Havok, Iron Man & Photon.  Kinda hate we picked Ruby, cause she was NO WHERE to be seen during both games. It be like that sometimes, I guess.

Rough. Needed that luck with piercing energy and the schemes at the right time to win this. First round was horrible with 8 escapes. Second time around I got the good cards early on and still it was fighting till the end. Doubt I would survive another Jean Grey chain as the villain deck was running out quickly.
Top 5 all time brain burner of a setup, and I have played this game 3-400 times easily
